name: Centrebank
description_short: CentreBank is a community bank founded in 1917 serving Indiana with checking, savings, loans, and mortgages across six locations.
description_long:
CentreBank, previously known as The Veedersburg State Bank, was established in 1917 by three families with 25 stockholders. The bank began with a single branch in Veedersburg, Indiana, and remained the only bank in that community for over 80 years. Since 1982, CentreBank has expanded strategically with new locations in Sterling (drive-up, 1982), Covington (1990), Kingman (2005), Hillsboro (2015), and Rockville (2024), growing from a single-branch institution to a six-location regional bank.

CentreBank offers a comprehensive suite of personal and business financial products. Personal services include checking accounts (Freedom Account, Silver Checking, Interest Bearing Checking), savings products (Money Market, Statement Savings, Christmas Club, Certificates of Deposit), and lending options (personal loans, home equity loans, auto loans, and residential real estate financing). Business customers can access business checking, business savings, CDs, and commercial lending including agricultural real estate financing, construction loans, and lines of credit. Digital banking services include online banking, mobile deposit, bill pay, automatic transfers, and a mobile app.

CentreBank distinguishes itself through its emphasis on community banking and relationship-based service. The bank's stated mission focuses on meeting community financial needs while maintaining personal and exceptional customer service. Their longevity (107 years of continuous operation) and local ownership structure differentiate them from larger regional or national banks. The bank explicitly positions itself as a "personal financial advocate" offering account aggregation tools and secure digital banking.

As a traditional community bank, CentreBank's primary limitations include limited geographic reach (six Indiana locations only) and likely higher fees or lower rates compared to larger institutions. No information is provided about specific APRs, fee structures, minimum balances, or comparative rates on deposit or loan products. Customers seeking nationwide branch networks or competitive online-only banking rates may find better alternatives. Many banks offer installment loans with fixed monthly payments, giving borrowers a clear payoff timeline.
services: Personal Checking (Freedom Account, Silver Checking, Interest Bearing Checking)
Personal Savings (Money Market, Statement Savings, Christmas Club, Certificates of Deposit)
Personal Loans and Home Equity Loans
Residential Real Estate Financing and Home Mortgages
Commercial or Agricultural Real Estate Financing
Commercial or Agricultural Term Loans and Lines of Credit
Online Banking, Mobile Deposit, Bill Pay, and Mobile App
pros: 107-year operating history (founded 1917) demonstrates stability and deep community roots
Six convenient branch locations across Indiana with drive-up service in Veedersburg
Offers full-service banking including personal and business checking, savings, loans, and mortgages
Mobile app and digital banking tools (mobile deposit, bill pay, account aggregation)
Multiple checking account tiers (Freedom, Silver, Interest Bearing) to suit different needs
Agricultural and commercial lending available for business customers
Certificate of Deposit products for both personal and business customers
cons: Limited to six locations in Indiana only—no geographic flexibility for customers outside the region
No published rates, APRs, or fee schedules available on website for transparent comparison
Smaller institution likely offers fewer investment products or wealth management services than larger banks
Website lacks information about deposit insurance limits, minimum balance requirements, or account protections
No mention of mortgage refinancing incentives, special loan programs, or competitive rate advantages
best_for: Indiana residents in Veedersburg, Covington, Rockville, Kingman, or Hillsboro seeking community banking
Small business owners and farmers needing agricultural or commercial lending with local relationship banking
Customers prioritizing long-term bank stability and personalized service over nationwide branch networks
Individuals seeking basic checking, savings, and mortgage products in rural Indiana communities
diagnosis: CentreBank is best for Indiana residents in its service areas (Veedersburg, Covington, Rockville, Kingman, Hillsboro) seeking stable community banking with personal service. The primary caveat is geographic limitation—the bank operates only six locations in Indiana, making it unsuitable for customers who need multi-state access or those outside these communities.
typical_results_timeline: Account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions vary by product.
